## Professional Experience

### Software Engineer — ContextQA (July 2025 – Present)
**Company:** https://contextqa.com
**Stack:** Next.js, TailwindCSS, Shadcn UI, Node.js, Express.js, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Prisma, Chrome Extensions

Shipped and maintained two production Chrome extensions with ~1,600–1,900 merged PRs each:

**Vibe Testing (~1,900 merged PRs):** AI-driven Chrome extension for automated web testing. Features real-time WebSocket log streaming, AI-generated bug reports with screenshots, integration with AI development platforms (v0, Lovable, Replit). Chat-based bug-fix workflows. Backend: Python AI agent + Node.js + Socket.io.

**AxeTos (~1,600 merged PRs):** Comprehensive WCAG A/AA/AAA accessibility testing Chrome extension. Features component-level violation visualization with precise DOM locators, intelligent fix suggestions, automated script-injection remediation via AWS S3. Backend: Node.js, Express.js, axe-core.

Also enhanced the ContextQA Portal backend using Node.js, Express.js, and Playwright — resolving critical bugs and implementing new features.

---

**Stack:** React.js, Node.js, Express.js, TailwindCSS, PostgreSQL, Supabase, OAuth, Stripe, Firebase

Key projects:
- **Brightmont (EdTech):** Education scheduling platform integrating Teachworks API with Python/Cplex scheduling optimization, AWS S3 cloud storage, real-time calendar UI with advanced data manipulation

**Stack:** HTML, CSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js, Express.js, React.js, Next.js, NestJS, MySQL, PostgreSQL, TailwindCSS, Redux, Sequelize, TypeORM

Owned authentication module end-to-end on Garage Management System team project (role-based access control, session management, user admin UI). Progressed from web fundamentals to production NestJS and Next.js in under 6 months.

## Projects

### EduScale
**Live:** https://eduscale.vercel.app/
**GitHub:** https://github.com/Shailesh93602/devscale
**Stack:** Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Supabase, Socket.io, @socket.io/redis-adapter, redlock, opossum, prom-client, Bull, Redis, Node.js, Express.js

Premium engineering learning platform with a production-grade distributed real-time engine:

**Architecture:**
- `@socket.io/redis-adapter`: Socket.io cluster adapter for horizontal scaling across multiple Node.js instances. All instances share room state via Redis.
- `redlock` (Redlock algorithm): Distributed lock manager preventing race conditions when two users simultaneously attempt to start the same battle. Lock is acquired before any battle state write.
- `opossum`: Circuit breaker wrapping the remote code-execution service. When the breaker trips (threshold exceeded), battles degrade gracefully rather than hanging.
- `prom-client`: Prometheus client exposing `/metrics` endpoint with active battle count, queue depth, and request latency histograms.
- `bull` queues: Reliable background job processing for code execution, result fan-out, and leaderboard updates.
- `winston`: Structured logging with correlation IDs across distributed operations.

**Features:** Dynamic Career Roadmaps, Real-time Battle Zone (<200ms sync), multi-language code execution, Community Forum, Placement preparation tracks, gamification.

---

### DevTrack
**Live:** https://daily-dev-track.vercel.app
**GitHub:** https://github.com/Shailesh93602/devtrack
**Stack:** Next.js, TypeScript, Prisma, PostgreSQL (Supabase), Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI, Recharts

Developer intelligence dashboard:
- Developer Scoring System (0–100 composite based on consistency, DSA depth, project productivity)
- Pattern Analysis detecting strongest/weakest problem-solving areas
- Prescriptive Recommendations engine suggesting high-yield daily tasks
- Visual Analytics: interactive Recharts, activity heatmaps, difficulty distribution (<300ms latency via Promise.all parallelization)

---

### KhataGO
**Live:** https://khatago.vercel.app/
**Stack:** Next.js, TypeScript, Prisma, PostgreSQL (Supabase), Gemini AI, WhatsApp Cloud API, Redis, Bull Queues, i18next, Node.js, Express.js
**Note:** Source code is private.

WhatsApp-first AI accounting platform for Indian SMBs:

**Technical depth:**
- **WhatsApp webhook deduplication:** Meta sends duplicate events. Each incoming message ID is hashed and checked against a Redis TTL window before processing — duplicates dropped in <1ms.
- **Gemini AI OCR pipeline:** WhatsApp image URL → download to buffer → Gemini Vision API → structured JSON (merchant, amount, date, line items) → ledger transaction. Error handling for low-confidence extractions.
- **Tally XML export:** Generates TALLYMESSAGE XML vouchers for accountants. Non-trivial: Tally requires specific date format, ledger name lookup from master data, and precise GST field structure. Wrong XML silently fails to import.
- **Bull queues:** All three operations (webhook processing, OCR, XML generation) are decoupled via Bull so the WhatsApp bot responds immediately (<500ms) while processing happens asynchronously.
- **i18next:** Full UI in English, Hindi, and Gujarati.

---

### CodeSenseiSearch
**GitHub:** https://github.com/Shailesh93602/CodeSenseiSearch
**Stack:** NestJS, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, pgvector, OpenAI Embeddings API

AI-powered semantic code search engine (Phase 7 complete — full pipeline working):

**Architecture:**
- **Ingestion:** Source files → function/class-level chunker (not fixed line counts) → OpenAI embeddings → pgvector storage
- **Search:** Natural language query → embedding → cosine similarity ranking → ranked file+line results
- **Key insight:** Chunking at function/class boundaries keeps semantic units intact. Fixed-size chunking splits logic mid-function, producing noisy embeddings.

Natural language queries like "where is the payment retry logic?" return ranked file paths and line ranges without keyword matching.

---

### redis-battle-demo
**GitHub:** https://github.com/Shailesh93602/redis-battle-demo
**Stack:** Node.js, Express.js, Socket.io, @socket.io/redis-adapter, Redlock, Redis, Docker Compose

Standalone proof-of-concept extracting the core distributed real-time engine from EduScale into a runnable demo:

**Architecture:**
- Two Redis clients: `pubClient` and `subClient` — separate clients required by `@socket.io/redis-adapter` for pub/sub isolation
- `@socket.io/redis-adapter`: All Socket.io room state synchronized through Redis; multiple server instances share a single socket namespace
- `Redlock` (retryCount:0): Distributed mutex on `battles:tick:lock` prevents two server instances from simultaneously emitting `server_tick`. Non-blocking — if lock is held, the tryTick silently skips rather than queuing
- `docker-compose.yml`: Single-command startup for Redis + demo server
- `public/index.html`: Browser UI showing live tick events and which server instance won the lock

Demonstrates the core concurrency problem in real-time multiplayer: without the distributed lock, two horizontally-scaled instances both emit the tick → clients receive duplicate events.

---

### CareerGlyph (WIP)
**Stack:** NestJS, TypeScript, Prisma, PostgreSQL, Swagger/OpenAPI

Developer identity platform — portable profile showing skills, projects, and endorsements. NestJS monorepo under `apps/backend/`:

**Prisma schema:**
- `Developer`: id, username (unique), name, bio, avatarUrl, isPublic, skills, projects, endorsementsGiven
- `Skill`: id, name, category (SkillCategory enum: FRONTEND/BACKEND/DATABASE/DEVOPS/OTHER), level (SkillLevel enum: BEGINNER/INTERMEDIATE/ADVANCED/EXPERT), endorsements
- `Project`: id, title, description, techStack (String[]), liveUrl, githubUrl, featured
- `Endorsement`: id, skill, giver — `@@unique([skillId, giverId])` prevents duplicate endorsements
- Cascading deletes on Skill/Project/Endorsement when Developer is deleted

**API:**
- `GET /profile/:username`: Returns developer with skills+endorsementCounts+projects. Throws 404 for unknown usernames and private profiles (isPublic=false). Swagger-annotated with `@ApiOperation`, `@ApiParam`, `@ApiResponse`.
- `@Global() DatabaseModule` exports `PrismaService` — single shared connection pool across all modules
- Seed file: sample developer, 3 skills (TypeScript/ADVANCED, React/ADVANCED, NestJS/INTERMEDIATE), 2 projects, 1 endorsement

---

### Vibe Testing (ContextQA — proprietary)
**Stack:** Chrome Extension, Python AI Agent, Node.js, Socket.io, WebSockets, TypeScript, Manifest V3
**PRs merged:** ~1,900

- Autonomous UI test generation and execution targeting AI-built apps (v0, Lovable, Replit)
- Real-time log streaming via Socket.io
- Screenshot capture at each test step

---

### AxeTos (ContextQA — proprietary)
**Stack:** Chrome Extension, Node.js, Express.js, axe-core, AWS S3, TypeScript
**PRs merged:** ~1,600

Professional WCAG accessibility testing suite:
- Automated WCAG A/AA/AAA auditing with precise DOM locators
- Component-level visualization of violations categorized by severity
- Intelligent fix suggestions with code snippets
- Persistent remediation via script injection (stored on S3, injected via Chrome Scripting API)
